notes: The game starts and works, but I do not reach more than 60 FPS on any settings, so the game is not playable.
title: War Thunder

notes:
Fresh Linux install. With the native client, game is capped at 60FPS and starts on the wrong monitor. Not an issue if you use windowed mode but then you must play at a smaller resolution. Vulkan workaround (changing config.blk settings to "vulkan") results in a black screen.

Proton doesn't work either, when I click play, nothing happens and the launcher closes.
title: War Thunder

notes: Launcher doesn't work. Got 150+ FPS with the Vulkan backend by changing the drivers in "config.blk" and running the game via win32/aces.exe - running the launcher will override your custom settings unless you set the file as read-only.
title: War Thunder

notes: Use Launch Option "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 "/path_to_your_library/War Thunder/win32/aces.exe" %command% -NoLauncher" to start directly, as hitting "play" in the launcher won't launch successfully. Runs mostly flawless with 3-4 times the FPS of the crippled native version.

title: War Thunder

notes: This appears to run fine now, however I did once get a false crash in background. There is one cavete' which might affect a select few people, and that is RESHADE does not work under steamplay, it causes the aces.exe to freeze. This is related with DXGI.DLL injector which allows you to run better fsaa and sharpening effects in game since War Thunder has pretty bad options for this.
title: War Thunder

notes: Native runs poorly (we are talking 30- fps on my 1060), but Proton has the potential to run well - but it doesn't work, crashing my whole system, I can't even access TTY when it goes down. I had the pleasure of playing 1 game out of 5 restarts.
title: War Thunder

notes:
When using Proton, the game crashes when joining a real match, but runs fine if you only play custom battles.
The native version runs perfectly, but has severe performance issues that make it somewhat unplayable.
When using Proton it also has much better performance than the native build.
title: War Thunder

notes:
The Native Client runs a LOT worse than on Windows so i force use Proton to run it wich is way better performance and options wise (on native client i'm limited to a max resolution of 1900x1200 wtf?). 
Overall Performance is very similar to Windows (besides some rare stuttering) and the only real Bug i encountered so far is that closing it, doesnt really work as it freezes the game window and after i force kill War Thunder, Steam crashes and i have to log out and log in wich is really anoying bc i play War Thunder a lot with my friends.
title: War Thunder

notes:
Windows client is A LOT more optimized then the native Linux one (50-60fps increase for me).

Issue is the game seems to be a bit unstable in Windows mode, mostly at the main menu. If desktop effects are enabled the screen will flash at the main menu and there is also stuttering lag that I have not been able to get rid off. Sometimes the game will also lock-up at the main menu if left to sit for too long. I keep a kill script on hand to force kill war thunder and then politely ask gaijin client to close..

Good news is once in the game everything seems to run very well. Advise for users I guess is if you want the FPS boost but are prepared to deal with these issues then go for proton. Otherwise stick with the native client.
title: War Thunder

notes: Steam Play/Proton offers better performance than the native linux client, first map has a couple ocorrences of stuttering, after that it gets smooth. Overall ingame experience is very good, however exiting always freezes the game, you need to manually kill gjagent.exe and aces.exe.
title: War Thunder

notes: The proton version of this game runs much better than the native port, which seems to have been abandoned by Gaijin. With everything maxed out and SSAA disabled I'm getting an average of 196.9 FPS on the Battle of Berlin Benchmark, while the native Linux version scored a ridiculously low 48 FPS. I did not observe any bugs or glitches, aside from minor stuttering in the main menu for about 10 seconds after boot.
title: War Thunder

notes: The native version has poor performance and occasional lagspikes/freezes that sometimes make the game very frustrating. With proton the game runs much better, but about half of the time it freezes when exiting battle forcing me to kill the process. I didn't try any tweaks.
title: War Thunder

notes: The native version's performance is terrible. Running with Proton provides easily double, sometimes triple the average framerate. Occasional menu interface flickering and one crash so far, but gameplay is smooth.
title: War Thunder

notes: After setting Vsync to "enabled", in the launcher, this game is perfect and there has been no more interface flickering or crashing. War Thunder runs as well as it does now on Windows (please remove last silver report).

title: War Thunder

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: EAC made the game unplayable with Proton, yet another great game ruined by lazy anticheat measures.
title: War Thunder

notes:
EAC isn't implemented yet, which means you can still play the game through Proton. Using these options, you don't need to set your config.blk file as read-only: 
gamemoderun "/mx500/SteamLibrary/steamapps/common/War Thunder/win64/aces.exe" %command% -safe -NoLauncher -noeac -forcestart -driver:vulkan
title: War Thunder

notes: If you are able to get into the game on Proton, this does NOT mean EAC is not active. It's just not active in your region. The game is slowly introducing EAC region by region, and just because your region isn't affected yet, doesn't mean it won't ever be affected. EAC is coming, and now you will have to wait in Valve Time for Valve to fix this with EAC. Game is officially borked as a result.
title: War Thunder

notes: Update 1.91.0.150 doesn't work with launch options to launch the game with Vulkan backend, safe mode, no EAC and no launcher. Gaijin is slowly and silently killing Linux and Proton support at every new update.
title: War Thunder
